Deere reports strong earnings, but weak outlook due to Trump tariffs impact; stock falls. Trump policies hit segments significantly.
news.summary: Deere reported earnings that surpassed expectations, but the company's segment outlook does not include the impact of Trump tariffs and retaliatory measures. The company anticipates a significant decrease in profits due to these tariffs. Consequently, Deere's stock declined following this announcement.

news.headline: Airbnb stock drops ahead of Q4 earnings report. Investors eager to view financial results.
news.summary: Ahead of the release of its fourth quarter earnings, Airbnb's stock experienced a decline on Thursday. Investors are eagerly anticipating the company's financial results. The exact figures that market participants are closely monitoring include revenue, profit, and any updates on key performance metrics. It is crucial to keep an eye on these numbers as they will provide important insights into Airbnb's financial health and overall performance.

news.summary: On Thursday, the Senate made the decision to appoint RFK Jr. to oversee health matters, raising speculation about his potential influence on biotech stock prices. This move has stirred interest in how he may shape the health sector.

news.headline: AppLovin stock soars after beating Q4 expectations. Investors ponder whether to hold APP stock post significant gain.
news.summary: Following its impressive Q4 report, AppLovin's stock saw a significant surge. The company's stock soared after beating expectations. With this substantial gain, investors may be wondering whether to hold onto their APP stock. Should they stick with it after the impressive performance in Q4?

news.headline: Higher infant mortality rates in states with abortion bans indicate a concerning trend.
Analyst discusses how 10-year Treasury yield affects Bitcoin's price, warns of challenges if inflation, unemployment rise.
news.summary: Cryptocurrency expert Benjamin Cowen recently delved into the implications of the latest Consumer Price Index (CPI) report on Bitcoin (BTC) and the overall market dynamics, highlighting the critical interplay between inflation and unemployment figures. During his most recent podcast, Cowen analyzed the impact of the 10-year Treasury yield on Bitcoin's pricing trends. He observed that Bitcoin encountered challenges in 2023 amidst rising yields, with recovery only transpiring post their peak. This indicates that Bitcoin might encounter obstacles if yields continue to ascend. Cowen also cautioned that a notable surge in inflation or unemployment could pose potential risks for the market. For a more comprehensive overview, visit the Benzinga.com platform.

Dow Jones falls due to Trump's new tariffs. Nvidia stock at key level. Fed Chair Powell's testimony is next.
news.summary: Tuesday saw a decline in the Dow Jones following President Donald Trump's implementation of new tariffs. Nvidia stock has reached a critical level that investors should monitor closely. Federal Reserve Chair Powell is scheduled to give testimony in the near future. The latest market developments highlight the impact of Trump's tariff policies, with the Dow Jones experiencing a notable drop. Powell's upcoming testimony and the positioning of Nvidia stock are key factors that investors are keeping an eye on.

news.summary: Detractors of President Donald Trump's trade policies caution that the implementation of significant new tariffs could result in a substantial increase in prices.

news.headline: Voyantis secures $41M funding, total now $60M. Tripled ARR twice. Intel Capital leads round.
news.summary: Tel Aviv-based Voyantis, a growth platform using AI for customer acquisition and optimization, recently secured $41 million in funding. This has elevated its total funding to $60 million. Over the past two years, Voyantis has tripled its ARR consistently. The funding injection is expected to accelerate its growth further. The investment round was spearheaded by Intel Capital and saw contributions from various entities like Square Peg, Target Global, Jibe, Redseed, Alicorn, 20Growth, Essential, and ICON. Lisa Coh, the Intel Capital Investment Director, played a significant role in the funding round.

Warren Buffett Indicator at 205%, S&P 500 P/E ratio over 22, warning of stock market bubble by veteran investor Howard Marks amid high valuations.
news.summary: The stock market is showing signs of a possible bubble, with the Warren Buffett Indicator reaching a high of 205%, surpassing levels before the 2000 dot-com crash and 2007 financial crisis. The S&P 500's forward P/E ratio is over 22, indicating overvaluation. Howard Marks, the veteran investor who predicted the dot-com crash, is cautioning about high market valuations in a recent memo. Marks pointed out various warning signs, including market exuberance, reliance on a few key stocks, and a bias towards index investing. These indicators suggest a bubble may be forming in the market.

Trump: We want to level playing field for all US companies.
Economists and betting markets predict mild January inflation: CPI to hold at 2.9%, with monthly increase of 0.3%.
news.summary: Economists and betting markets are currently predicting a mild January inflation report. The Consumer Price Index is expected to remain at 2.9% year-over-year, with a projected monthly increase of 0.3% (down from December's 0.4% rise). Core inflation, excluding food and energy prices, is anticipated to ease to 3.1% year-over-year, with a monthly increase of 0.3%. Markets are currently pricing in one rate cut by the end of the year, while there is a 50% probability of two cuts by December 2025. Betting markets similarly favor a 2.9% CPI reading, with odds of inflation exceeding 2.9% standing at 2-to-1. An unexpected inflation rate above 3.0% could yield a $53 return on a $10 wager.

news.headline: Fed Chair Powell says no hurry to cut interest rates, inflation somewhat elevated. Job gains average 189k/month, unemployment at 4%.
news.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell recently addressed policymakers' stance on interest rates, emphasizing a cautious approach due to persistent inflation pressures. Despite making progress towards the Fed's 2% inflation target, Powell stated there is no rush to make adjustments. Speaking before the Senate Banking Committee, Powell acknowledged the strength of the economy, noting a monthly average of 189,000 payroll job gains over the last four months and an unchanged unemployment rate of 4% in January. Powell reassured that the labor market, although cooled, remains robust and not a significant source of inflationary pressures. He highlighted a decline in inflation over the past two years but noted that inflation levels are still somewhat elevated. The latest data revealed a 2.6% year-over-year increase in the Personal Consumption Expenditures price index as of December, with core PCE, excluding food, rising by 2.8%.

Databricks seized AI opportunity amid Snowflake and Palantir rivalry, intensifying competition.
news.summary: The competition between two enterprise software companies, Snowflake and Databricks, grew stronger with the rise of generative artificial intelligence technology. Databricks seized the opportunity in AI amidst the rivalries with Snowflake and Palantir.

news.headline: Registration now open for Sui Overflow 2025 hackathon, a global event offering 8 weeks of blockchain innovation.
news.summary: Sui Overflow 2025, a prestigious worldwide hacking competition, is calling for teams and individuals to sign up for an eight-week program dedicated to pioneering blockchain initiatives. This event is set to gather numerous participants from around the globe to collaborate on cutting-edge projects in the blockchain industry. If you are seeking to be part of this groundbreaking event, make sure to register soon to secure your spot in this exciting opportunity.

news.headline: US stocks dip as Powell remains cautious on rates. Intel climbs 7%, Tesla sinks to 11-week low. Markets await inflation data. Dow gains 0.1%.
news.summary: U.S. stocks slightly dropped as Federal Reserve Chair Powell expressed no rush to cut interest rates, affecting risk assets. Investors awaited the January Consumer Price Index report. S&P 500 fell by 0.1%, Nasdaq 100 by 0.2%, but Dow Jones gained 0.1%. Intel Corp. rose 7% post announcement on U.S. AI chips, while Tesla Inc. fell 4% due to weak Chinese sales. EU planned countermeasures against 25% U.S. tariffs, leading to higher long-dated Treasury yields at 4.54%. Dollar index fell by 0.2%. Full news on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Mike Lindell Media Corp adds TV personality Vanessa Broussard to its News Media Agency team post President Trump rally shooting.
news.summary: Mike Lindell Media and FrankSpeech Network are making significant strides in expanding their news media agency by welcoming Vanessa Broussard, a new news media personality. This marks a continued momentum in the growth of the network, highlighting their commitment to enhancing their news media offerings. The addition of Broussard underscores the network's dedication to providing diverse perspectives and quality content to its audience. This development showcases the network's dedication to expanding its reach and influence in the media landscape.

news.headline: New tariffs of 25% on steel and aluminum could elevate manufacturing costs for various sectors in the U.S.
news.summary: A 25 percent tariff on steel and aluminum will impact various industries, including car buyers, beer drinkers, home builders, and oil drillers who use these metal goods.

news.summary: A record-breaking 1,500 new COVID-19 cases were reported in the city today, bringing the total number of active cases to 10,000. Hospital admissions have also surged, with 300 new patients being admitted for treatment. Additionally, the death toll has risen to 150 as 20 new fatalities were reported. The local government has implemented stricter safety measures in response to the concerning spike in cases.

Greenheart Gold expands Majorodam project in Suriname by 151.64 sq. km. Drilling begins at Heuvel target.
news.summary: In Longueuil, Quebec on February 13, 2025, Greenheart Gold Inc. (TSXV: GHRT) has expanded its exploration rights at the Majorodam project in Suriname. The original project covered 99.85 sq. km., but following agreements with two different title holders, it has now grown by 60.78 sq. km. to the north and 90.86 sq. km. to the south, totaling 251.5 sq. km. On February 12th, 2025, a reverse circulation drill rig arrived at the site, and drilling has begun at the Heuvel target.
